retry.notes: Erase the solid white filling the nose and redraw the nose exactly as the pen outlines it: a thin chalk outline around the nose shape with a DARK fillable interior, tracing the shine ellipse as a thin outline too — never leave the nose a solid white shape. FIX THE EYES — the polarity inside each eye is currently backwards: the LARGE oval filling most of each eye is the PUPIL and it must be DARK, not white. Turn each large white pupil oval dark, keeping only a thin white outline around it, and paint the SMALL dot inside it (the catchlight, currently dark) SOLID WHITE instead. The thin crescent between the eye's outer ring and the pupil stays solid white as the pen leaves it. After the edit, the only white inside each eye is that thin crescent, the pupil's thin outline, and the tiny catchlight dot. Keep the rump patch on the dog's back an OUTLINED fillable region exactly as the pen draws it — trace its boundary as a thin line, never fill it solid white. The open mouth contains ONLY the tongue outline — do NOT draw teeth or any white shapes inside the mouth. Do not whiten or fill solid any other region and do not add any new marks anywhere.

chalk.review: Fixed 2026-07-14 (ISSUES Tier 1): the 2026-07 wave chalk had solidified the pen's solid-ink nose AND the merely-outlined rump patch into solid white, whitened both solid pen pupils (white orbs, dark catchlight dots — the police-wide class), and INVENTED a row of teeth in the open mouth (the duck-wide class; the pen has only a tongue). All of that measures white 0.0% — chalk white sitting on pen solid ink is not 'new ink' — so every gate passes; the only machine signal is the 'eye whites not chalked' warning, whose ABSENCE marked the good take. The retry notes are the take-3 winner: 3.1 fixed nose/patch/mouth from erase-and-redraw phrasing on take 1 but resisted the eye edit until the note described the polarity swap geometrically (the LARGE oval is the PUPIL and must be dark); a shorter 'keep the nose…' paraphrase on take 2 regressed the nose back to solid — keep the full erase phrasing for every region. Judge on the chalk-over-night COMPOSITE, whole chalk vs pen, not just the reported regions; after a chalk regen, regenerate the night fill and re-punch. Good eye treatment matches sibling dog-tall: solid white eyeball, dark fillable pupil, white catchlight.
chalk.why: 2026-07-13 dark-mode composite review filed it; fixed 2026-07-14 ISSUES Tier 1 session (chalk take 3 of 3 at t 0.4, keep 100%/local 99.4%).

retry.notes: Keep the puppy's daytime identity colors, just moonlit-dimmed: a warm golden-brown coat (never navy, never grey), with the muzzle and the rump patch on its back a LIGHTER warm cream-tan clearly distinct from the coat and from the sky, a deep blue collar, a warm gold tag, and a dusky warm pink-red tongue inside the mouth. Paint the nose interior deep near-black and each eye's pupil interior deep near-black so the white catchlight reads clearly. No part of the dog may be painted the same navy as the sky.

night.review: Conditions on the 2026-07-14 fixed chalk (outlined nose, dark pupils, outlined patch, toothless mouth). Confirm on the composite: near-black nose and pupils, cream-tan rump patch and muzzle distinct from coat and sky, golden-brown coat matching the light identity, no teeth. First pinned take shipped (drift 0.0013, bgLuma 38, lineW 255, pre-ship haloScore 0.742 / rawScore 1.542 against the new chalk).
night.why: 2026-07-14 ISSUES Tier 1 fix — night regenerated against the fixed chalk; palette pinned to the light identity per the ISSUES night-contrast trap, t 0.4 because the notes combine region-darkness instructions with palette pins.

retry.notes: Erase the solid black inside each pupil and redraw it: white sclera, a black outlined pupil ring, and a small white catchlight inside the pupil. Never leave a pupil as solid ink — solid chalk ink renders as glowing white in dark mode.

chalk.review: Solid-pen-eye page: the pen's pupils are solid ink, the shipped 2026-07 chalk copied them, and the composited night eyes read washed-out white with no legible pupil (ISSUES Tier 1). findEyeCores finds nothing here, so the eye-polarity gate passes VACUOUSLY — render the chalk-over-night composite and confirm dark pupils before trusting any chalk of this page. After a chalk regen, regenerate the night fill and re-punch.
chalk.why: 2026-07-13 dark-mode composite review — same class and recipe as vehicles/police-wide. 3.1 resists erase-style edits on solid ink; budget extra attempts.

retry.notes: Erase the solid black inside the pupil and redraw it: white sclera, a black outlined pupil ring, and a small white catchlight inside the pupil. Never leave a pupil as solid ink — solid chalk ink renders as glowing white in dark mode.

chalk.review: Solid-pen-eye page: the pen's pupil is solid ink, the shipped 2026-07 chalk copied it, and the composited night eye is a blank white orb (ISSUES Tier 1). findEyeCores finds nothing here (0 cores), so the eye-polarity gate passes VACUOUSLY — render the chalk-over-night composite and confirm a dark pupil before trusting any chalk of this page. After a chalk regen, regenerate the night fill and re-punch.
chalk.why: 2026-07-13 dark-mode composite review — same class and recipe as vehicles/police-wide. 3.1 resists erase-style edits on solid ink; budget extra attempts.

light.review: A flat-eye flag here is the known side-profile detector false positive — verified lively by eye in the 3.1 wave (gemini-3.1-migration.md). judgeLightEyes has no suppressions (ISSUES), so adjudicate visually before burning retries.
light.why: The night judge's IDEAS #12 suppressions cleared this page's night-side flag; the light judge still re-derives cores and misreads the side-profile eye.

retry.notes: the open bill interior must stay toothless — the pen has a clean open bill with a tongue outline and no teeth; do not draw teeth. The duck is drawn in side profile with exactly ONE eye, already present on the head — do NOT add a second eye, an extra eyeball, or any new facial mark anywhere (not above the bill, not on the bill); redraw ONLY the lines the pen already has

chalk.review: Fixed 2026-07-13: the 2026-07 wave chalk had INVENTED a row of human-like teeth inside the open bill (the pen and light fill have none). The toothless note alone was not enough — the first regen take removed the teeth but invented a SECOND EYEBALL (sclera + pupil + catchlight) above the bill, inside the head interior where the enclosure gate reads whitening as deliberate; the one-eye guard in the retry notes is what cleared it (take 2). Check the bill interior AND the whole head for extra eyes on the chalk-over-night COMPOSITE after any regen; every gate passes an invented face.
chalk.why: 2026-07-13 ISSUES Tier 1 fix (chalk-invented facial features) — the eye-flavored chalk instruction invents features gate-blind inside the subject; regenerated chalk + night, re-punched.

retry.notes: The open bill contains ONLY a tongue outline — do NOT paint teeth or any white shapes inside the bill; the mouth interior stays deep warm red-brown with the tongue slightly lighter. The duckling keeps its warm golden-yellow body with an orange bill and orange feet — never navy, never grey.

night.review: Conditions on the 2026-07-13 toothless chalk. Confirm on the composite: toothless open bill, golden-yellow duckling matching the light fill's identity, lively side-profile eye. First pinned take shipped (drift 0.0019, bgLuma 34, lineW 255, haloScore 0.026).
night.why: 2026-07-13 ISSUES Tier 1 fix — night regenerated against the de-toothed chalk; palette pinned to the light identity per the ISSUES night-contrast trap.
